page: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server allocates 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled (2 threads per core) on a dedicated basis. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX-512, and AES-NI instruction sets. It includes 524 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between compute and memory capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. Multi-core performance is average, with a Passmark CPU score of 7667.57 and a multi-core stress-ng result of 6562.94. However, memory bandwidth remains solid, achieving 366.56 GB/sec for cached reads and approximately 49.76 GB/sec for uncached reads. Database operations and Redis SET operations both perform in the average tier, with Redis handling 6.41 million operations per second.

With 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this server offers high memory density relative to its compute resources. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand substantial memory capacity but do not require intensive, high-frequency CPU processing. It is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and enterprise applications with large working sets. It is less suitable for CPU-bound tasks, high-throughput web serving, or machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v3 is an ESv3-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz, it includes 524 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. While single-core CPU benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 25%, its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 6.41 million operations per second. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-frequency compute power. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and local storage for databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
